title: Club Isola in Basel closes
description: The gay club Isola at Gempenstrasse 60 in Basel shut its doors, ending its run as a meeting place for the local scene.

title: Basel youth group Rose disbands
description: The gay youth group Rose in Basel dissolved toward the end of the year. The youth group grenzenlos took over as its successor.

title: Zurich lesbian counselling service moves into the HAZ Centro
description: The Lesbenberatung counselling service, active in Zurich since 1993, relocated to the Centro of the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich (HAZ).

title: Final issue of spot news appears
description: The newsletter spot news, published by the Zurich youth group spot 25, released its last issue around the start of 2004.

title: HAZ launches the gayAgenda listings publication
description: The Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich replaced their previous bulletin Schwall with the expanded Zurich gayAgenda, produced together with the Bern group HAB and appearing ten times per year.

title: Youth group Grenzenlos forms in Bern
description: A gay, lesbian and bisexual youth group named Grenzenlos started up in Bern. It remained active until June 2005.

title: HuK Zurich dissolves
description: The Zurich chapter of HuK, the group for homosexual people in the churches, disbanded. The Basel and Bern chapters continued their work.

title: Lugano authorities refuse to welcome the Pink Cross assembly
description: Pink Cross held its general assembly in Lugano. For the first time since the organization's founding, the host city's authorities declined to officially greet the delegates.

title: spot25 becomes a working group of the HAZ
description: The Zurich youth group spot25 was integrated into the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich as one of its working groups.

title: Romandy alliance for the partnership law forms in Sion
description: Ten LGBTQ organizations from French-speaking Switzerland — among them Alpagai, Dialogai, Lestime, 360° and VoGay — joined forces in Sion as the Association romande Oui au partenariat enregistré to campaign for the registered-partnership law.

title: Queer history exhibition travels to Basel
description: The exhibition on same-sex love first shown in Zurich in winter 2002/2003 went on display in Basel, with several sections adapted to cover the local Basel context.

title: Youth group Expect starts in St. Gallen
description: Expect, a group for homosexual and bisexual young people up to age 26, formed in St. Gallen.

title: Fribourg voters approve constitution recognizing same-sex partnership
description: In a cantonal referendum, voters in Fribourg adopted a new constitution containing an article on same-sex partnership by 44,864 votes to 32,446, replacing the constitution of 1857.

title: Fribourg constitution with partnership article takes effect
description: The new Fribourg cantonal constitution, which includes an article on same-sex partnership, entered into force.

title: Panel on queer topics in schools held in Zurich
description: The HAZ school working group organized a panel discussion on lesbian, bisexual and gay issues in schools at the Provitreff in Zurich, with Zurich education director Regine Aeppli among the participants.

title: Basel conference on sexual orientation in the workplace
description: A diversity conference on diversity management and sexual orientation at work took place in Basel, organized by Rolf Stürm, followed by a public evening panel moderated by Patrick Rohr.

title: akut: magazin debuts as successor to aK
description: The first issue of akut: magazin appeared, continuing the publication previously known as aK.

title: Council of States approves the registered-partnership bill
description: The Swiss Council of States passed the federal bill on registered partnership for same-sex couples by 25 votes to 0, with 4 abstentions, moving the legislation a step closer to adoption.

title: Zurich CSD marches under the banner Rights Now!
description: Zurich's Christopher Street Day parade took place with the motto "Rights Now! - Lesben & Schwule weltweit", putting the global rights situation at its center.

title: Martin Naef heads a cantonal party as its first openly gay president
description: Martin Naef was elected president of the Social Democratic Party of the canton of Zurich, the first openly gay man to lead a Swiss cantonal party.

title: Swiss parliament passes the registered-partnership law in its final vote
description: The federal registered-partnership law for same-sex couples cleared its final vote in the Swiss parliament, completing the legislative process before the referendum challenge that followed.

title: Neuchâtel cantonal partnership law takes effect
description: The cantonal partnership law of Neuchâtel entered into force, giving same-sex couples in the canton a form of legal recognition ahead of the federal law.

title: Pride Romande held in Geneva
description: Geneva hosted the Pride Romande, a week of Lesbian & Gay Pride events culminating in a large parade on 10 July.

title: Rapperswil gay film festival ends after twelfth edition
description: The twelfth edition of the gay film days in Rapperswil turned out to be the festival's last.

title: Anniversary conference 30 Jahre LesBiSchwul cancelled
description: A conference planned at Boldern to mark thirty years of the lesbian, bisexual and gay movement had to be called off for lack of interest.

title: Referendum against the partnership law secures enough signatures
description: The EVP, EDU, Swiss Democrats and parts of the SVP submitted almost 67,000 signatures in Bern against the registered-partnership law, forcing a national referendum on the legislation.

title: First Miss Gay crowned alongside Mister Gay in Zurich
description: The first Miss Gay election, held together with the Mister Gay contest, drew a large crowd to the Zurich Kongresshaus. Both titleholders pledged to campaign for the federal partnership law.

title: Mass demonstration for the partnership law in Bern
description: A large demonstration in support of the registered-partnership law took place in Bern ahead of the referendum campaign.

title: Awareness group La Boussole forms in Romandy
description: La Boussole, a group raising awareness of sexual orientation and diverse identities, formed in French-speaking Switzerland, affiliated with Pink Cross and the lesbian organization LOS.

title: Bern city pension fund to treat same-sex couples equally
description: Following a 54-16 vote in the Bern city parliament, the municipal pension fund resolved to grant same-sex couples equal treatment starting in 2006.

title: Bern high court extends victim-support law to same-sex partners
description: The high court of the canton of Bern ruled that the victim-assistance law also applies to partners in same-sex couples.

title: Basel-Stadt anchors same-sex partnerships in pension law
description: The canton of Basel-Stadt wrote recognition of same-sex partnerships into its new pension-fund law.

title: Gay Fricktal group forms
description: Homosexual women and men from the Fricktal region of Aargau, the Basel countryside and adjacent areas came together as the group Gay Fricktal.

title: Winterthur group Wisch reorganizes as Wilsch
description: The Winterthur group Wisch reconstituted itself as Wilsch, now covering lesbians as well as gay men, and its club room Derwisch took the new name Badgasse 8.

title: Gay Film Rapperswil-Jona emerges from Klick
description: The group Klick in Rapperswil-Jona reorganized itself under the name Gay Film Rapperswil-Jona.

title: Queer Kinok film screenings return to St. Gallen
description: St. Gallen saw queer film screenings for the second time after 1999, now presented under the name Queer Kinok.

title: St. Gallen bear club renamed Säntis-Gays
description: The bear club of St. Gallen and eastern Switzerland adopted the new name Säntis-Gays.

title: First Checkpoint health center opens in Geneva
description: The Geneva organization Dialogai opened the first Checkpoint, a health center for gay men and other men who have sex with men.

title: Queerofficers Switzerland founded
description: Homosexual officers of the Swiss armed forces formed the association Queerofficers Switzerland.

title: European gay and lesbian manager association egma founded in Vienna
description: Six national associations of gay and lesbian professionals — network from Switzerland, Völklinger Kreis, Wirtschaftsweiber, L'Autre Cercle, PrIMO and AGPro — established the European Gay and Lesbian Manager Association (egma) in Vienna.

title: Campaign for the partnership law opens with Bern media conference
description: The committee Ja zum Partnerschaftsgesetz held a media conference in Bern to launch its campaign for the referendum on the registered-partnership law.

title: Partnership-law campaign officially launched in Willisau and Lucerne
description: A major event in Willisau and at the Hotel Schweizerhof in Lucerne marked the official opening of the voting campaign Ja zum Partnerschaftsgesetz.

title: Zurich CSD rallies for a yes to the partnership law
description: Zurich's Christopher Street Day took place under the referendum-focused motto "Go for YES!" one day before the national vote on the partnership law.

title: Swiss voters approve the registered-partnership law
description: In a national referendum, 58 percent of Swiss voters approved the federal law on registered partnership for same-sex couples, with turnout at 55.7 percent. Switzerland thereby became the first country to confirm partnership rights for same-sex couples by popular vote.

title: National Lesbian & Gay Pride held in Lucerne
description: Lucerne hosted the Swiss national Lesbian & Gay Pride under the motto Sowieso, two weeks after the partnership-law referendum.

title: network opens eastern Switzerland and Liechtenstein section
description: The gay professionals' organization network founded its fifth section in St. Gallen, covering eastern Switzerland and Liechtenstein.

title: European LGBT youth exchange held in Liechtenstein and Switzerland
description: A European LGBT youth exchange under the motto "To break taboos" brought young people together in Schaan-Vaduz in Liechtenstein and in Davos and Zurich in Switzerland.

title: Bern hosts the exhibition Unverschämt unterwegs
description: Bern became the third city to show the queer history exhibition Unverschämt unterwegs, in a version adapted to the local Bern context.

title: Youth group Happy gays forms in Neuchâtel
description: Happy gays, a lesbian, bisexual and gay youth group offering exchange, counselling and support, started up in Neuchâtel.

title: Claude Janiak elected as first openly gay National Council president
description: Claude Janiak of the Social Democrats in Basel-Landschaft was elected president of the Swiss National Council for 2006, the first openly gay man to hold the office.

title: Second Pink Flamingo festival takes place in Basel
description: Basel hosted the second edition of Pink Flamingo, a lesbian and gay festival.
